scenario: Greenfield Node.js project — lock framework and runtime versions
expected_behavior: Skill runs runtime version commands, records exact output (e.g., 'v18.17.0', 'npm 9.8.0'), presents complete tech stack with pinned versions, awaits user approval before writing lock files
notes: Runtime verification is mandatory first step — output goes into RULES.md under 'Runtime Environment'

scenario: Compatibility conflict — user requests versions that are incompatible
expected_behavior: Skill detects version mismatch, alerts user immediately with specific requirement, blocks lock file generation, waits for user to resolve conflict
notes: Early detection prevents Stage 5 SAST failures from version mismatch

scenario: Python project — missing explicit version specification
expected_behavior: Skill runs 'python --version' (or 'python3 --version'), records actual runtime version, generates requirements.txt with pinned dependencies (FastAPI==0.95.2, sqlalchemy==2.0.19, etc.), presents for approval
notes: Must execute runtime command before proposing versions — never assume 'latest'

scenario: Monolithic vs microservices mismatch — tech stack conflicts with architecture
expected_behavior: Skill flags architecture mismatch, asks user to clarify intent: (1) monolithic should use simpler stack, (2) architecture decision should change to distributed
notes: Prevents downstream Stage 3 design failures caused by misaligned tech decisions
